title: macos: running podman run ... from inside a subshell breaks the terminal newlines
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

This morning I encountered a very peculiar behavior from podman when it is run from inside bash subshell, basically it breaks the terminal after the command ends.

I most of the day trying to debug what happens, assuming it might be caused by a shell extension, my zsh or something else that is user specific. Still, the everika moment was when I tried to use docker instead and that one did not break the terminal at all.

For sure the problem happens on MacOS with both zsh and bash and what happens is that every newline after that acts like a teletype line-feed (LF) instead of of full newline.

I was not able to reproduce the problem with podman on Linux!

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

PS. I am still debugging the issue, so I might get back with more details soon. Still, I needed a place where to document this weird behavior, maybe someone else also encountered it.

comments.body: This is likely caused by `-t`, try removing it from your podman run/create commands.

I can confirm that `-t` worked. Still I would like to investigate why its presence messed the terminal after running. The same option from docker does not do that and I also have some other cases where I need to run it interactively from a script, so I still need to find the root cause.

For example I dumped of shell vars before and after the run to compare if one of them might cause this and I did not see anything to be worried about.

What other commands should I run to debug it?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@Luap99 Any other thoughts on this one?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm closing this due to a lack of action and adding -t was confirmed to work.  feel free to request it be reopened
